blocking_constraints.description: Laboratory lacks a twin‑screw extruder capable of maintaining ~260 °C zones and an appropriate screw design (e.g., double‑flight or mixing section) for processing high‑viscosity Fortron® PPS while simultaneously grafting maleic anhydride onto polypropylene.
blocking_constraints.suggested_mitigation: Acquire or upgrade a lab‑scale twin‑screw extruder with 260–280 °C barrel temperature capability, suitable screw geometry, and sufficient residence time; alternatively use a reactive compounding setup or co‑extrusion line that can handle the required temperatures.

system3.feedback_to_system2: Exclude candidates that require twin‑screw extrusion above 260 °C without confirmed lab equipment, and verify precursor safety handling before proposing synthesis.

blocking_constraints.description: KEBAPEAK PEEK XS1300 and the epoxy‑functional copolymer compatibilizer lack documented lab‑scale availability or certification for FDA food contact and RoHS compliance.
blocking_constraints.suggested_mitigation: Identify alternative suppliers that can provide certified 10–100 g quantities, or develop in‑house synthesis routes with verified safety data.

blocking_constraints.description: Regulatory compliance for the composite (FDA 21 CFR Part 177 extractable limits, RoHS, PFAS/BPA/phthalate content) is not confirmed.
blocking_constraints.suggested_mitigation: Obtain a full chemical analysis and regulatory certification from the material supplier or conduct in‑house testing to verify compliance.

blocking_constraints.description: Critical processing parameters (MFI at extrusion temperature, residence time, pressure, atmosphere) are missing, preventing assessment of co‑extrusion feasibility and mechanical performance at 200 °C.
blocking_constraints.suggested_mitigation: Perform experimental melt flow measurements and process trials to establish these parameters before scaling up.

system3.feedback_to_system2: Prioritize candidates with confirmed lab‑scale availability, documented regulatory compliance, and available processing data (MFI, residence time, pressure) to avoid infeasible proposals.

system2.candidate: RILSAN® BMNO + ENGAGE™ 030902 HEALTH+ Polyolefin Elastomer Composite (with MA‑grafted polyolefin tie resin and epoxy‑fun

blocking_constraints.description: The composite requires a twin‑screw extruder with ≥260 °C zones and a mixing section for reactive compatibilization, which is not available in the laboratory.
blocking_constraints.suggested_mitigation: Acquire or access a suitable high‑temperature twin‑screw extruder, or explore alternative processing routes such as melt blending in a lab furnace.

blocking_constraints.description: Critical processing parameters (MFI at extrusion temperature, residence time, pressure profile) are not documented, preventing assessment of co‑extrusion feasibility.
blocking_constraints.suggested_mitigation: Conduct pilot studies or obtain detailed process data from the supplier.

blocking_constraints.description: FDA 21 CFR Part 177 and RoHS compliance for the epoxy‑functional copolymer compatibilizer is not confirmed; medium confidence only.
blocking_constraints.suggested_mitigation: Request formal compliance letters from the supplier.

blocking_constraints.description: Lab‑scale availability and regulatory certification of the epoxy‑functional copolymer compatibilizer are not documented.
blocking_constraints.suggested_mitigation: Verify with supplier or identify an alternative compatible additive.

system3.feedback_to_system2: Avoid proposing composites that require twin‑screw extrusion above 260 °C when the lab lacks such equipment, and ensure all components have documented lab‑scale availability and confirmed regulatory compliance.
